descriptions: The Outlaws amalgamated the High Spirits Motorcycle Club to form their West Side chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The West Side chapter was formed in 1994 when the Outlaws amalgamated the High Spirits Motorcycle Club in Bensenville

Joe Boys.description: The Joe Boys were a Chinese American youth gang founded in the 1960s in San Francisco's Chinatown by Joe Fong, a former Wah Ching member. They were involved in drug trafficking, extortion, robbery, and murder. The gang was active from 1969 to 1977 and had conflicts with the Wah Ching and Hop Sing Tong.

descriptions: The infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as a rival of the Joe Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wah Ching , Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: The Joe Boys and Wah Ching were engaged in a violent war.
edges.evidence_quote: By 1973, the struggle between the Chung Ching Yee and the Wah Ching had erupted into a war that had claimed 13 lives since 1969

descriptions: Wah Ching was involved in the Golden Dragon Massacre with the Joe Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Wah Ching first received widespread media attention because of the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre (GDM) involving another Chinese gang, the Chung Ching Yee, or Joe Boys (JBS).

descriptions: Infobox lists Chung Ching Yee as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Asian Boyz, Bamboo Union, Black Dragons, Chung Ching Yee, Wo Hop To

descriptions: The Joe Boys originated as a splinter from the Yau Lai.
edges.evidence_quote: Joe Fong's group had splintered from the main Yau Lai in early 1971

descriptions: George Cornell moved from the Watney Streeters to ally with the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cornell was originally a member of an East End gang called "The Watney Streeters" and later became involved with the Krays. However, he changed sides around 1964 and allied himself with the Richardsons.

Cartel del Dorado.description: Colombian criminal organization active from 1970 to 2010, based in Bogotá. It supplied narcotics to the Medellín Cartel and later became one of the world's largest after the decline of the Medellín and Cali cartels. Led by Justo Pastor Perafán and later Luis Caicedo, it engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, human trafficking, and arms trafficking. The organization was dismantled after Caicedo's arrest in 2010.

descriptions: Cartel de Bogotá is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cartel de Bogotá

descriptions: Cartel del Dorado's leader had drug trafficking relations with Cartel de Cali despite enmity between Medellín and Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Perafán tuvo relaciones narcos tanto con el cartel de Medellín como el de Cali, a pesar de la enemistad entre ambos.

descriptions: Cartel del Dorado supplied drug traffickers to Cartel de Medellín and had mutual collaboration; some members joined Medellín's structures.
edges.evidence_quote: En las décadas de 1980 y 1990, el cartel suplía de narcotraficantes al cartel de Medellín . Había colaboración mutua y varios de los miembros del cartel pasaron a formar parte de las estructuras del cartel de Medellín.

descriptions: Justo Pastor Perafán led both Cartel del Dorado and Cartel del Cauca simultaneously.
edges.evidence_quote: Uno de los más prominentes líderes del cartel fue el narcotraficante Justo Pastor Perafán , organización que dirigía a la par con el Cartel del Cauca, basado en la ciudad de Popayán .

descriptions: Elephant and Castle Mob was allied with the Camden Town gang.
edges.evidence_quote: They were allied to the Birmingham gang, often called the Brummagems, led by Billy Kimber; the Camden Town gang, led by George Sage and the Finsbury Boys, led by Freddie Gilbert.

Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida).description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Florida by ex-US naval servicemen. Known as a 'One Percenter' club with chapters in the US, Canada, England, and Germany. The mother chapter remains in Orlando, Florida. Founder Tom 'Grub' Freeland died in 2019.

descriptions: Warlocks listed as rival of Kings Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida)

descriptions: Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) listed as a rival of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Grim Reapers , Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida) , EPS , RCMP , Canadian Airborne Regiment

descriptions: The Boozefighters were in talks to patch over into the Warlocks.
edges.evidence_quote: as well as in talks with the Boozefighters about patching over into the Warlocks

descriptions: The Warlocks were in merger talks with the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Chaffin was in talks to merge interests with the Hells Angels down in Florida

descriptions: The Warlocks have a rivalry with the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: The Warlocks have a rivalry with the Outlaws and the Pagans.

descriptions: The Warlocks have a rivalry with the Pagans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Warlocks have a rivalry with the Outlaws and the Pagans.

descriptions: The Florida Warlocks are a rival gang to the Warlocks.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival gangs include the Breed and the Florida Warlocks.

descriptions: De Luca Bossa and Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno were opposed in a long bloody feud.
edges.evidence_quote: La lunga e sanguinosa faida che ha visto contrapposte negli anni le famiglie De Luca Bossa e Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno

descriptions: Gruppo Annacondia was affiliated with Alessandro Modeo's clan.
edges.evidence_quote: infatti Annacondia non aveva aderito alla SCU voluta da Rogoli, preferendo rimanere affiliato solo con Cosa nostra e con Alessandro Modeo

descriptions: Gruppo Annacondia remained independent from Sacra Corona Unita, not joining it.
edges.evidence_quote: Come quello tarantino dei Modeo, ha avuto una certa indipendenza dalla Sacra Corona Unita: infatti Annacondia non aveva aderito alla SCU voluta da Rogoli, preferendo rimanere affiliato solo con Cosa nostra e con Alessandro Modeo

Aldington Gang.description: A band of smugglers active in the Romney Marshes and shores of Kent, England, founded around 1817. They used The Walnut Tree Inn as their headquarters and were known for large-scale smuggling operations involving spirits, tobacco, and salt. The gang was likely the last major smuggling gang in Kent and was known as 'The Blues' from the color of their clothing or signal flares.

Cheetham Hill Gang.description: Organised crime group based in Cheetham, Manchester, England, established by the early 1980s. Engaged in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, and other crimes. Known for turf wars with Moss Side and Salford gangs, and for controlling Manchester's club scene. At its peak between the late 1980s and early 2000s, still active as of present.

descriptions: Cheetham Hill Gang was in a turf war with Pepperhill Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1980s and 1990s, the Cheetham Hill Gang was involved in a turf war with Moss Side gangs, particularly the Pepperhill Mob and later Doddington Gang.

descriptions: Cheetham Hill Gang was in a turf war with Doddington Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1980s and 1990s, the Cheetham Hill Gang was involved in a turf war with Moss Side gangs, particularly the Pepperhill Mob and later Doddington Gang.

descriptions: Gooch Close Gang bought and sold drugs to Cheetham Hill, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Later, The Gooch Close Gang often bought and sold drugs to Cheetham Hill. The Gooch had close ties to Cheetham Hill, with the cousin of the Cheetham Hill Gang's head being a leading figure in the Gooch.

descriptions: Doddington gang was rival of Cheetham Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang became rivals of the Cheetham Hill Gang and the Gooch Close Gang during the late 1980s and early 1990s

descriptions: Pepperhill Mob and Cheetham Hill Gang were in violent conflict over drug territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pepperhill Mob, rich and well armed despite their youth, fought back. The two gangs vied with each other in stabbings, tit-for-tat shootings and murder

descriptions: Pepperhill Mob was in a gang war with Cheetham Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s and early 1990s, the Pepperhill Gang were involved in a gang war between Moss Side and the Cheetham Hill Gang from North Manchester.

descriptions: Gooch Close Gang was aligned with Cheetham Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gooch, aligned with the Cheetham Hill Gang and the larger Westside Gooch faction, started a violent turf war

descriptions: Gooch Close Gang cooperated with Cheetham Hill Gang through drug trade and close ties.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gooch often bought and sold drugs with the Cheetham Hill Gang. The Gooch had close ties to Cheetham Hill

descriptions: Infoboox lists Cheetham Hill Gang as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cheetham Hill Gang

descriptions: The Noonan family forged links with the Cheetham Hill Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Damian and Desmond Noonan were forging links with other notable Manchester gangs including the Cheetham Hill and Salford gangs.

descriptions: The Vallelunga are historically allied with the Turrà.
edges.evidence_quote: Storicamente alleati dei Turrà di Guardavalle

descriptions: The Croxteth Crew killed a member of the Norris Green Strand Crew, indicating a violent rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The murder came the day before the first anniversary of the killing of Liam Smith, an alleged member of a rival gang, the Norris Green Strand Crew, who was shot dead by members of the Croxteth Crew on 23 August 2006.

descriptions: Los Dragones is a rival of Los Ciclones per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Metros , Cártel del Noreste , Los Dragones , Sinaloa Cartel

Bahala Na Gang.description: The Bahala Na Gang is a Filipino street gang founded in the early 1940s in Sampaloc, Manila, by Divino Talastas. It originated as a protective group against rival gangs and later expanded into criminal activities including drug and weapons trafficking. The gang spread to the United States and remains active, with alliances including Nuestra Familia.

descriptions: Infobox lists Bahala Na Barkada as a rival of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Satanas , Bahala Na Barkada , Pinoy Real

descriptions: Infobox lists Nuestra Familia as an ally of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuestra Familia , True Brown Style

descriptions: Infobox lists Pinoy Real as a rival of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Satanas , Bahala Na Barkada , Pinoy Real

descriptions: Infobox lists Satanas as a rival of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Satanas , Bahala Na Barkada , Pinoy Real

descriptions: Bahala Na Gang was formed to counter the Sige-Sige Gang, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: To counter the existing Sige-Sige Gang, Divino formed the Bahala Na Gang

descriptions: Infobox lists True Brown Style as an ally of Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuestra Familia , True Brown Style

descriptions: Infobox lists Bahala Na Gang as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bahala Na Gang

descriptions: Satanas has a rivalry with Bahala Na Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Avenues , Bahala Na Gang

descriptions: Bahala Na Gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bahala Na Gang

Águilas Negras.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization formed after the demobilization of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC). It was involved in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and terrorism. The group emerged in 2006 in Norte de Santander and expanded to other departments. It was led by Daniel Rendón Herrera (alias Don Mario) and was largely dismantled by 2014, with remnants joining the Clan del Golfo.

descriptions: Black Eagles is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Águilas Negras formed from former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Águilas Negras is a successor neoparamilitary group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Águilas Negras is a successor group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Black Eagles are a successor group to AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: However, local successors such as the Black Eagles continue to exist and death threats have been made using its name.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo absorbed former members of Águilas Negras.
edges.evidence_quote: el grupo asumió todas las actividades criminales del exnarcotraficante Daniel Rendón Herrera , alias Don Mario , impulsor de varias organizaciones al margen de la ley (hoy día inactivas y cuyos exmiembros pasaron a ser parte del Clan del Golfo); entre ellas las Águilas Negras

descriptions: Some members of Águilas Negras were part of the demobilization of AUC, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Algunos de sus miembros formaron parte de la desmovilización de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Black Eagles formed from preexisting AUC forces after demobilization.
edges.evidence_quote: Black Eagles ... emerged from the failures of the demobilization process between 2004 and 2006, which aimed to disarm the United Self-Defense Units of Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Black Eagles were closely linked with Usuga Clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Black Eagles were one of a number of groups formed following the demilitarisation of the AUC, and were said to be closely linked with the Usuga Clan drug cartel and right-wing neo-paramilitary group.

descriptions: Remnants of Águilas Negras joined the Clan del Golfo, indicating absorption.
edges.evidence_quote: los reductos que aún quedaban se anexaron a la banda criminal del Clan Úsuga (hoy día llamada Clan del Golfo)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is listed as a group under Black Eagles.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups - Los Rastrojos: operating in Cauca and Valle del Cauca (approximately 1,200 members).

descriptions: Mano Negra is listed as a group under Black Eagles.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups - Mano Negra: operating in Putumayo (unknown number of members).

Cartel de la Costa.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization active from the 1980s to 1998, based in the Caribbean region of Colombia. It controlled drug trade routes to the Caribbean and Central America, initially operating autonomously and later as a franchise of the Medellín and Cali cartels. The group was led by José Rafael Abello Silva and later Alberto Orlández Gamboa. It dissolved in 1998 due to arrests, deaths, and the incursion of the AUC paramilitary group.

descriptions: Cartel de la Costa is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cartel de la Costa

descriptions: Bloque Norte de las AUC took over the Costa cartel's drug routes after its dissolution.
edges.evidence_quote: El Bloque Norte de las AUC se apropió de las rutas del narcotráfico del cartel de la Costa

descriptions: North Coast Cartel hired AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is believed to have employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as a terrorist organization, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: The infobox lists Cartel de Cali as an enemy until its dismantlement.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel del Norte del Valle , Cartel de Cali (Hasta su desarticulación)

descriptions: Cartel de Cali assigned Caribbean routes as a territorial franchise to the Costa cartel's leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Al caer el cartel de Medellín, el cartel de Cali asignó las rutas del Caribe como franquicia territorial a Orlández Gamboa.

descriptions: The Costa cartel's second-in-command was a partner of the Guajira cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Salomón Camacho Mora alias "Papa Grande": Segundo al mando del Cartel de la Costa y socio del Cartel de La Guajira

descriptions: A lieutenant of the Costa cartel also led the Tierra cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Libardo de Jesús Parra González , alias "El Flaco" o "El Guajiro": Lugarteniente de Orlández Gamboa, también lideró el "Cartel de la Tierra".

descriptions: Cartel de Medellín assigned the Caribbean region as a territorial franchise to the Costa cartel's leader.
edges.evidence_quote: con la irrupción de poder del cartel de Medellín , Escobar asignó la región Caribe como franquicia territorial al "Mono" Abello.

descriptions: Cartel del Norte del Valle took over territory from the weakened Costa cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: su territorio empezó a ser copado por otros agentes ilegales, y narcotraficantes en su mayoría ligados al cartel del Norte del Valle

descriptions: The infobox lists Cartel del Norte del Valle as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel del Norte del Valle , Cartel de Cali (Hasta su desarticulación)

descriptions: Norte del Valle Cartel killed a member of the North Coast Cartel and his brother.
edges.evidence_quote: Gustavo Salazar Bernal : Killed in Cartagena on August 30, 2001, by gunman Jhon Fredy Orrego Marín who was associated to the Norte del Valle Cartel . He was killed for being responsible for "losing" a shipment of drugs property of "Los Mellizos" with destiny to Europe. His brother Fernando Salazar Bernal was also killed by the Norte del Valle Cartel .

descriptions: Alberto Orlández Gamboa, leader of the Costa cartel, was a partner of the Nasser Arana clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue socio del clan Nasser Arana junto con Roger Pombo Ortega, Germán Libonatty Pimienta alias "Pedro", Jattin Arnulfo Pinto Vásquez y Julio César Anicchiarico.

descriptions: The Costa cartel's leader eliminated the Valdeblanquez clan in a vendetta.
edges.evidence_quote: Orlández Gamboa eliminó al Clan Valdeblanquez en una vendetta.

descriptions: The Costa cartel allied with former police to strengthen Los Alcatraces.
edges.evidence_quote: Gamboa y su lugarteniente en el cartel Wildron Gabriel Daza Mejía, alias "Gabi Daza" (asesinado en 2011 ), realizaron alianza con expolicías para potenciar bandas criminales como "Los Alcatraces"

South Side Compton Crips.description: The South Side Compton Crips are a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Compton, California, formed in the 1970s. They are primarily African-American and known for their involvement in the murder of Tupac Shakur in 1996. The gang operated numerous drug houses during the crack epidemic and has ongoing rivalries with various Piru and Crips sets.

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Elm Street Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: South Side Compton Crips , Kelly Park Compton Crips , Santana Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Southside Compton Crips is a rival of Fruit Town Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Southside Compton Crips

descriptions: Mob Piru and South Side Crips are rival gangs in Compton.
edges.evidence_quote: Shakur, Knight, and Death Row Records were associated with the Mob Piru set of the Bloods from Compton. Allegedly, before the murder, a Southside Crip, whom are a rival gang to the Mob Piru gang in Compton

descriptions: Mob Piru Bloods and South Side Compton Crips were in a gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: a gang war broke out in Compton between the Mob Piru Bloods, and the South Side Compton Crips

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Mob Piru in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: South Side Compton Crips

descriptions: Infobox lists South Side Compton Crips as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: South Side Compton Crips

descriptions: Atlantic Drive Compton Crips is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Atlantic Drive Compton Crips

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips member Orlando Anderson confronted members of Corner Poccet Crips, leading to a gunfight.
edges.evidence_quote: Anderson and his friend Michael Dorrough confronted Michael Stone and Jerry Stone, two members of the Corner Poccet Crips

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips is a subset of the Crips gang alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The South Side Compton Crips (also known as the South Side Crips ) are a " set " of the Crips gang alliance

descriptions: Fruit Town Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Fruit Town Piru

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips members shot at a vehicle they believed was from Lime Hood Piru Bloods, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The two gang members believed they were protecting a drug house from a rival gang, the Lime Hood Piru Bloods.

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips attacked a member of Mob Piru, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: a group of South Side Compton Crips led by Orlando Anderson attacked Trevon Lane, a Mob Piru Blood

descriptions: Mob Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru

descriptions: Neighbourhood Compton Crips is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Neighbourhood Compton Crips

descriptions: Nutty Blocc Compton Crips is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nutty Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Santana Blocc Compton Crips is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Santana Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham fought alongside Tawhid Brigade against Ghuraba al-Sham Front.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2013, Ahrar al-Sham alongside Al-Nusra, ISIL, and the Tawhid Brigade fought the Ghuraba al-Sham Front because of looting and corruption on behalf of Ghuraba al-Sham as well as disputes Ghuraba al-Sham had with the Aleppo Sharia Court.

descriptions: The Derry Boys were a junior subgroup of the Billy Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: The Billy Boys also formed a junior group whose members were teenagers called the Derry Boys.

descriptions: The Billy Boys and Norman Conks frequently clashed due to sectarian and territorial rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: the Protestant Bridgeton (or Billy) Boys who frequently clashed with the Roman Catholic Norman Conks in the East End of the city

descriptions: The Billy Boys were formerly known as Penny Mobs.
edges.evidence_quote: two of the most infamous were the former Penny Mobs , the Bridgeton Billy Boys and the Norman Conks

descriptions: The Norman Conks and Billy Boys were engaged in street fighting, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They were often involved in street fighting with the Protestant Billy Boys of Bridgeton

descriptions: Infobox lists Atlantic Drive Compton Crips as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Atlantic Drive Compton Crips

descriptions: Atlantic Drive Compton Crips is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Atlantic Drive Compton Crips

descriptions: D'Alessandro cooperates with Di Martino in Emilia-Romagna.
edges.evidence_quote: In collaborazione con il clan Di Martino, è attivo anche in Emilia-Romagna

The Carlton Crew.description: The Carlton Crew is a criminal organization based in Melbourne, Australia, founded in the 1970s by Gino Rosace. It is named after the Melbourne suburb of Carlton and has been involved in racketeering, murder, drug trafficking, and other crimes. The group played a significant role in the Melbourne gangland killings and has been a rival to Calabrian 'Ndrangheta groups. As of 2019, Tony Madafferi is the boss.

descriptions: Honoured Society and Carlton Crew have close cooperation from mid-1990s to present.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Carlton Crew (close cooperation from the mid-1990s to the present)

descriptions: The Moran family is allied with the Carlton Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Carlton Crew, Federated Ship Painters and Dockers Union

descriptions: Carlton Crew had a strong rivalry with Calabrese Family.
edges.evidence_quote: The organisation had a strong rivalry with the Honoured Society and the Calabrese Family, both of which were Calabrian 'Ndrangheta groups also based in Melbourne.

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was a fierce rival to the Calabrese Family.
edges.evidence_quote: The organisation was a fierce rival to the Honoured Society and the Calabrese Family

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was rivals with the Calabrese Family until the early 90s.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Williams Syndicate, Honoured Society and Calabrese Family (until the early 90s)

descriptions: The Carlton Crew became allies with the Calabrese Family since the mid-90s.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Honoured Society and Calabrese Family (since the mid-90s)

descriptions: The Carlton Crew became allies with the Honoured Society since the mid-90s.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Honoured Society and Calabrese Family (since the mid-90s)

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was allied with the Moran family.
edges.evidence_quote: and were additionally allies of the mostly Irish Moran family

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was allied with Radev Bratva.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Honoured Society and Calabrese Family (since the mid-90s), Moran and Pettingill families, Radev Bratva

descriptions: Carl Williams switched allegiance from the Carlton Crew to the Williams Family in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: Allegiance: The Carlton Crew (Until 2000) , The Williams Family (2000-2007)

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was rivals with the Williams Syndicate.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Williams Syndicate, Honoured Society and Calabrese Family (until the early 90s)

descriptions: PAC was renamed to RYO.
edges.evidence_quote: the PAC's name was changed to Revenge Youth Organisation (RYO).

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Caballeros Templarios as an enemy of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Grupo Flechas, Cártel del Noreste, Cártel de Guasave, Cártel Nueva Plaza, Guerreros Unidos, Artistas Asesinos, Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima, La Familia Michoacana, Los Caballeros Templarios, Los Talibanes, Unión Tepito, Cártel de los Soles, Los Choneros

descriptions: Junky Funky Kids is a rival of The Americans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rivals of the Americans gang include all gangs affiliated to the British gang corporate body. These include the Hard Livings , 28s street gang, Junky Funky Kids, Ghetto Kids, Nice Time Kids, The Terrible West Siders, The Fancy Boys, 6bop gang, Mongrels, Young Gifteds and Terrible Josters.

descriptions: 3-N-G feuded with Calliope Porch Boyz in early 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2000s they were known to feud with another Central City gang called the "Calliope Porch Boyz".

Los Viagras.description: Los Viagras is a Mexican criminal group based in Michoacán, founded around 2014 as a self-defense group and later evolving into a drug trafficking organization. Led by Nicolás Sierra Santana, the group is involved in methamphetamine production and transportation, and has been in conflict with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel. They are known for using minors as human shields and generating violence in the Tierra Caliente region. As of 2024, they have allied with the CJNG.

descriptions: CJNG and Los Viagras are in a war, with a violent attack in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: El 25 de abril de 2020, asaltan un campamento clandestino en Aguililla, Michoacán. Aparentemente el ataque fue parte de la guerra entre el CJNG y Los Viagras.

descriptions: CJNG fights Los Viagras in Michoacán and Guerrero.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting ... Los Viagras ... for the states of Michoacán and Guerrero

descriptions: CJNG is in conflict with Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting ... Los Viagras ... for the states of Michoacán and Guerrero

descriptions: CJNG is in conflict with Los Viagras in Michoacán and Guerrero.
edges.evidence_quote: The CJNG are fighting ... Los Viagras ... for the states of Michoacán and Guerrero

descriptions: Jalisco New Generation Cartel is fighting Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: The Knights Templar Cartel are now allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and are fighting the La Nueva Familia Michoacana Organization and its armed wing Los Viagras

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Viagras as an ally of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Tijuana, Cártel del Golfo, Los Metros, Los Chapitos, Los Viagras, Cártel de Caborca, Fuerza Anti-Unión, Los Lobos

descriptions: La Resistencia allied with Los Viagras to fight CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: La Resistencia made an alliance in 2015 with Los Viagras

descriptions: Cárteles Unidos lists Los Viagras as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Familia Michoacana , Los Viagras , La Nueva Familia Michoacana , Los Blancos de Troya , Los Correa

descriptions: Los Viagras is a member organization of Cárteles Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: Cárteles Unidos, conocido inicialmente como La Resistencia, es una organización criminal mexicana compuesta por miembros del ... Los Viagras

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Viagras as an enemy of Guerreros Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Gobierno de México, Ejército Mexicano, Armada de México, Cártel de Sinaloa, Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación, Cartel del Golfo, La Familia Michoacana, Los Viagras, EPR.

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Sierra as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como Los Ciclones , Los Metros , Los Escorpiones , Los Rojos , Los Sierra o División Sierra , Los Ceros, Fuerzas Especiales Grupo Sombra (F.E.G.S.), Los Zetas , entre otros.

descriptions: Knights Templar Cartel is fighting Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: The Knights Templar Cartel are now allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and are fighting the La Nueva Familia Michoacana Organization and its armed wing Los Viagras

descriptions: Caballeros Templarios considers Los Viagras as enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Viagras

descriptions: Los Viagras are a cell or splinter of the Caballeros Templarios.
edges.evidence_quote: diferentes células de los Caballeros Templarios entre ellos «los viagras»

descriptions: La Familia Michoacana is in conflict with Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Infighting between La Familia Michoacana and La Nueva Familia Michoacana Organization and its armed wing Los Viagras is ongoing.

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Viagras as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Viagras

descriptions: La Familia Michoacana allied with Los Viagras to fight CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: La Familia Michoacana se ha visto obligada a aliarse con Los Viagras y los reductos de Los Caballeros Templarios, para hacer frente al CJNG.

descriptions: Los Viagras is the armed wing of La Nueva Familia Michoacana Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: La Nueva Familia Michoacana Organization and its armed wing Los Viagras

descriptions: Beltrán-Leyva Cartel is listed as a defunct ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Los Viagras disputed territory with CJNG before their alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: disputándose el territorio contra el Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG).

descriptions: Los Viagras and Jalisco New Generation Cartel are in a turf dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Viagras expanded into the drug trade, first by becoming involved in the production and transportation of methamphetamine, which brought them into a turf dispute with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, particular the Tierra Caliente region of Michoacán.

descriptions: Los Viagras allied with CJNG in August 2024 to control extortion and fight Cárteles Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: En agosto de 2024 se confirmó que Los Viagras se aliaron con el CJNG, sobre todo por el control de las extorsiones contra los productores de aguacate y limón, así como la lucha compartida contra Cárteles Unidos.

descriptions: Infobox lists CJNG as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación

descriptions: Los Viagras has links with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Viagras, which has links with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel

descriptions: Los Viagras allied with CJNG to fight Los Caballeros Templarios and Los de Tepeque.
edges.evidence_quote: las disputas internas terminaron por orillarlos a aliarse con el CJNG

descriptions: Los Viagras has both ties and rivalry with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Viagras (which is now known to have ties and rivalry with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel).

descriptions: Infobox lists Cártel de Los Correa as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Los Correa

edges.target: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: Infobox lists Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima

descriptions: Los Viagras and Cartel del Abuelo are allied against Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Viagras and the Cartel del Abuelo were reported as having a "profound advantage" over New Generation in Tierra Caliente.

descriptions: Los Viagras and Cartel del Abuelo had an advantage over CJNG together.
edges.evidence_quote: se sabe que Los Viagras y el "Cartel del Abuelo" tenían una "ventaja profunda" sobre el cártel Jalisco en Tierra Caliente.

descriptions: Los Viagras and CJNG jointly fought against Cárteles Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: así como la lucha compartida contra Cárteles Unidos.

descriptions: Infobox lists Cárteles Unidos as an enemy since 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Cárteles Unidos (desde 2024)

descriptions: Infobox lists Cárteles Unidos as an ally until 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cárteles Unidos (hasta 2024)

descriptions: Los Viagras and H3 are rival splinter groups fighting for territorial control.
edges.evidence_quote: diferentes células de los Caballeros Templarios entre ellos «los viagras» y los «h3» se disputan el control territorial

descriptions: Infobox lists Cártel Independiente de Acapulco as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Cártel Independiente de Acapulco

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Acapulco is listed as a defunct ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Knights Templar Cartel is listed as a rival of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, La Barredora, Knights Templar Cartel

descriptions: Los Viagras assisted in capturing the leader of Los Caballeros Templarios.
edges.evidence_quote: Former Michoacán security commissioner Alfredo Castillo Cervantes then asked them to assist in the efforts to capture the leader of the Los Caballeros Templarios cartel, Servando Gómez Martínez ("El Tuta"), who was then arrested in Morelia in February 2015.

descriptions: Guardia Michoacana is listed as an ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Caballeros Templarios as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Los Caballeros Templarios

descriptions: Los Viagras formed after the dissolution of the Knights Templar Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Knights Templar Cartel was eventually defeated and dissolved by the vigilantes' actions but they ended up forming their own cartel called Los Viagras

descriptions: Los Viagras originated as dissidents from Los Caballeros Templarios.
edges.evidence_quote: disidentes de Los Caballeros Templarios y la Familia Michoacana.

descriptions: La Barredora is listed as a rival of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, La Barredora, Knights Templar Cartel

descriptions: Infobox lists La Familia Michoacana as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... La Familia Michoacana

descriptions: Los Viagras originated as dissidents from La Familia Michoacana.
edges.evidence_quote: disidentes de Los Caballeros Templarios y la Familia Michoacana.

descriptions: La Nueva Familia Michoacana is listed as an ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Infobox lists La Nueva Familia Michoacana as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: La Nueva Familia Michoacana

descriptions: Los Viagras is a sub-unit of the LNFM Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Viagras ... is an armed wing of the LNFM Cartel in the state of Michoacán, Mexico.

descriptions: Los Blancos De La Troya is listed as an ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Los Blancos de Troya is the armed wing of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: siendo comandante del brazo armado "Los Blancos de Troya" Los Viagras los meses posteriores.

descriptions: Los Viagras fought against Los de Tepeque.
edges.evidence_quote: en su lucha para combatir a Los Caballeros Templarios y "Los de Tepeque" otra banda delictiva local

descriptions: Infobox lists Cártel de Sinaloa as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Pueblos Unidos formed to defend against Los Viagras, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: It began in 2021 as a self-defense group defending the local avocado farmers from cartel gangs Los Viagras and Jalisco New Generation

descriptions: Yakuza is allied with Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación , Los Viagras , Operativo Sierra Leona , Cartel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Torcasio is allied with Giorgi-Pizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Torcasio è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Lamezia Terme alleata dei Gualtieri, dei Cerra e dei Giorgi-Pizzata di San Luca

descriptions: The two sets were in a conflict that led to a mass shooting in Sacramento in 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: the shooting was related to a conflict between the local Garden Blocc Crips and Del Paso Heights Bloods gangs

descriptions: The Corleonesi were rivals of the Bontade faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Stefano Bontade

descriptions: Evil Ones is a puppet club of Hells Angels in Drummondville.
edges.evidence_quote: Every Quebec region had its own puppet club: the Rockers Motor Club in Montreal, the Rowdy Ones in Sorel, the Evil Ones in Drummondville, the Satan's Guard in the Saguenay region, and the Jokers in St-Jean

descriptions: Evil Ones is a puppet club of the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: the South Shore clubhouse of the Evil Ones, a Hells Angels puppet club

descriptions: Evil Ones are a puppet gang of the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: all of the Rockers and all of the Evil Ones puppet gangs
